source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Encryption at Rest and in Transit is configured

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Encryption at Rest and in Transit is configured
input_row.description: Enabling encryption at rest and in transit for Amazon ElastiCache helps protect your data when it is stored and transmitted.
input_row.rationale: Enabling encryption at rest secured the users data where it is stored. Enabling encryption in transit helps that the data is protected when it is moving from one location to another. Impact: If the user didn’t enable encryption and rest and during transit, there is a possibility of the data being vulnerable to a ransomware attack.
input_row.audit: 1. Enable Encryption at Rest • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon ElastiCache console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/elasticache/. • Create a new ElastiCache cluster or select an existing cluster. • On the cluster details page, click the Encryption tab. • Select the option to enable encryption Under the Encryption at Rest section. • Choose the desired encryption type: o list text hereDefault Encryption: Select this option to use the default AWS- managed key for encryption. o list text hereCustomer Managed Key (CMK): Select this option to use your own AWS Key Management Service (KMS) customer-managed key for encryption. • If you selected Customer Managed Key (CMK), choose the appropriate KMS key from the dropdown menu. • Click "Save changes" to enable encryption at rest for the ElastiCache cluster. 2. Enable Encryption in Transit • On the ElastiCache cluster details page, click the Encryption tab. • Select the option to enable encryption Under the "Encryption in Transit" section. • Choose the desired encryption type:  o list text hereTransit encryption enabled with SSL/TLS: Select this option to enable encryption in transit using SSL/TLS encryption. o list text hereTransit encryption disabled: Select this option if you do not require encryption in transit. • Click Save changes to enable encryption in transit for the ElastiCache cluster. 3. Verify the Encryption Status • Wait a few minutes for the changes to propagate and the encryption to take effect. • Refresh the ElastiCache console and navigate to the cluster details page. • Verify that the encryption status is now enabled for both encryptions at rest and in transit.
input_row.remediation: The user has two options when it comes to encryption at rest and in transit to choose from. Depending on what actions the user selects from it determines how their data is going to be protected. References: 1. https://aws.amazon.com/products/databases/
input_row.impact: If the user didn’t enable encryption and rest and during transit, there is a possibility of the data being vulnerable to a ransomware attack.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control involves verifying encryption settings for Amazon ElastiCache clusters, specifically checking if encryption at rest and encryption in transit are enabled. These are standard configuration properties for AWS services like ElastiCache. These properties can be verified using AWS provided APIs, making this an automated check.

**DECISION CRITERIA:**
✓ Can AWS APIs query this property? → Yes, ElastiCache supports relevant APIs for encryption configuration.
✓ Is this a standard AWS service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Yes, encryption settings.
✓ Does boto3 SDK have methods to check this? → Yes, methods are available to describe ElastiCache clusters.
✓ Does AWS CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Yes, AWS CLI can be used to describe and list ElastiCache configurations.
✓ Does it require SSH/RDP to instances? → No, encryption settings are configured at the AWS service level.
✓ Does audit mention boto3/AWS CLI commands? → While not explicitly, the operations described are achievable with AWS CLI/boto3.
✓ Is it about IAM, S3, EC2, VPC, CloudTrail, Config, KMS, etc.? → Relevant to KMS and ElastiCache cluster configuration.
✓ Is it about application code, OS configs, or visual review? → No, it's about checking AWS service configuration.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_elasticache_cluster_encryption_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use boto3's `describe_cache_clusters()` to list all ElastiCache clusters.
- For each cluster, retrieve properties related to `CacheClusterId`.
- Check if `TransitEncryptionEnabled` and `AtRestEncryptionEnabled` are both set to `True`.
- Validate against desired KMS settings to ensure they use either AWS-managed keys or customer-managed keys, based on compliance requirements.
- The AWS CLI equivalent command can be: `aws elasticache describe-cache-clusters --show-cache-node-info`.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass if both `TransitEncryptionEnabled` and `AtRestEncryptionEnabled` are `True`.
  - Fail if either or both are `False`.
- This ensures data is protected both in transit and at rest, aligning with the compliance requirements.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
